description:

            feelings, habits, strengths, and life perspectives, fostering deeper self-understanding and emotional intelligence.

description:
Use these prompts to deepen present-moment awareness, contact, and embodied experience. Gestalt questions often
            orient the client to "here and now" sensations, emotions, and relational patterns.

title: Crisis & Safety Questions
description:
Use these prompts to assess risk, identify protective factors, and build a short-term safety plan. Follow local protocols
            and organizational policy when risk is present.

THERAPY SESSION QUESTIONS  How do you perceive the balance between effort and support in the therapeutic process?  What are your expectations regarding the duration or length of therapy?  How open are you to exploring and challenging your existing beliefs or perspectives in therapy?  How do you anticipate therapy will impact your emotional well-being and self-awareness?  What role do you envision yourself playing in the therapeutic process?  How do you see therapy helping you build new coping strategies and skills?  What are your expectations regarding the confidentiality and privacy of therapy sessions?  How do you imagine therapy will help you gain insights and understanding into your own thoughts, feelings, and behaviors?  What are your expectations regarding the level of involvement and engagement required from you in therapy?  How do you anticipate therapy will support you in creating positive changes in your life?  How open are you to exploring and addressing potential blind spots or areas of growth in therapy?  What are your expectations regarding the therapeutic relationship and the level of trust and rapport you hope to develop with your therapist?  How do you see therapy facilitating your selfacceptance and self-compassion?  How do you anticipate therapy will impact your ability to manage stress and navigate difficult emotions?  What are your expectations regarding the frequency and scheduling of therapy sessions?  How do you envision therapy providing you with tools and strategies for effective communication and problem-solving?  How open are you to receiving feedback and guidance from your therapist in therapy?  What are your expectations regarding the level of support and validation you hope to receive in therapy?  How do you see therapy helping you develop a greater sense of clarity, purpose, and fulfillment in your life? THERAPY PROGRESS REFLECTION  How do you perceive your progress since starting therapy?  What changes have you noticed in your thoughts, emotions, or behaviors since beginning therapy?  In what ways have you applied the insights or strategies learned in therapy to your daily life?  How have your relationships with others evolved or improved as a result of therapy?  What challenges or obstacles have you encountered during your therapeutic journey, and how have you navigated them?  How have your goals or priorities shifted or become clearer as therapy has progressed?  What patterns or themes have emerged in your therapy sessions that you find significant or noteworthy?  How has your understanding of yourself and your experiences deepened through the therapeutic process?  How have you developed a greater sense of selfawareness or self-compassion in therapy?  What strengths or personal resources have you discovered or tapped into during therapy?  How have you integrated new coping strategies or skills into your daily routine as a result of therapy?  What insights or breakthroughs have you experienced in therapy that have been particularly meaningful to you?  How have you become more attuned to your needs and boundaries through the therapeutic process?  In what ways have you cultivated a greater sense of resilience or inner strength in therapy?  How has therapy helped you challenge and reframe unhelpful beliefs or thought patterns?  What progress have you made in terms of managing stress or navigating difficult emotions? THERAPY SESSION QUESTIONS  How have you developed a greater capacity for self-reflection and introspection in therapy?  What feedback or observations have you received from others that indicate your growth or progress in therapy?  How has therapy influenced your overall wellbeing and quality of life?  How have you become more skilled at setting and pursuing meaningful goals in therapy?  What insights have you gained about the underlying causes or triggers of your challenges or concerns?  How has therapy supported you in making healthier choices or taking positive actions in your life?  What evidence or indicators suggest that you are moving closer to your desired outcomes in therapy?  How have you cultivated a greater sense of acceptance and self-compassion through therapy?  In what ways have you developed a more balanced perspective or a more nuanced understanding of your experiences in therapy?  What changes have you noticed in your communication style or interpersonal dynamics as a result of therapy?  How have you grown in your ability to identify and assert your needs and boundaries in therapy?  What insights have you gained about the impact of past experiences on your current thoughts, feelings, and behaviors?  How have you become more adept at recognizing and managing unhelpful patterns or triggers in therapy?  How do you envision continuing to build upon the progress you have made in therapy to create a fulfilling and meaningful future? ENDING A SESSION  How would you describe your overall experience and journey in therapy?  What significant insights or realizations have you gained through the therapeutic process?  How have you grown or changed as a result of engaging in therapy?  What goals or outcomes have you achieved during the course of therapy?  In what ways have you developed new coping strategies or skills that you can carry forward?  How do you envision maintaining the progress you have made in therapy outside of the therapeutic setting?  What strategies or resources have been most helpful to you in achieving your therapeutic goals?  How have you developed a greater sense of selfawareness and self-acceptance in therapy?  What have been the most meaningful or impactful moments or experiences in therapy for you?  How has therapy influenced your relationships and interactions with others?  What challenges or obstacles have you overcome during therapy, and how have they contributed to your growth?  How do you plan to continue nurturing your emotional well-being and self-care after therapy?  What have you learned about yourself and your patterns of thinking, feeling, and behaving that will support your continued personal growth?  How have you developed a more balanced and resilient mindset through therapy?  How have you cultivated a greater sense of purpose, meaning, or fulfillment in your life as a result of therapy?  How have you strengthened your ability to set and maintain healthy boundaries in therapy?  What shifts or changes have you observed in your perception of yourself and your place in the world? THERAPY SESSION QUESTIONS  How have you become more skilled at identifying and challenging unhelpful beliefs or thought patterns?  What support systems or resources will you continue to rely on as you move forward from therapy?  How have you developed a greater sense of agency and empowerment in your life through therapy?  What aspects of your identity or self-concept have evolved or become clearer in therapy?  How has therapy contributed to your overall wellbeing and quality of life?  What feedback or observations have you received from others that indicate your growth or progress in therapy?  How do you plan to integrate the lessons and insights from therapy into your daily life?  What advice or encouragement would you offer to someone who is considering therapy?  How do you plan to maintain a commitment to self-care and personal growth as you transition from therapy?  How have you developed a greater capacity for self-compassion and self-acceptance through therapy?  What skills or strategies have you developed for managing stress and navigating difficult emotions?  How do you envision using the knowledge and skills gained in therapy to contribute positively to your community or society?  What hopes or aspirations do you hold for your future, knowing the progress you have made in therapy?
